session.h(3) Library Functions Manual session.h(3)

NAME

session.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct ring_consensus_t
Ring consensus state (Host-Mediated Proactive Election). struct host_liveness_t
Host liveness detection state. struct migration_ctx_t
Host migration context for tracking failover state. struct discovery_session_t
Discovery session context. struct discovery_config_t
Configuration for discovery session.

Macros


#define MAX_PARTICIPANTS 16

Typedefs


typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)
Callback to check if session should exit.

Enumerations


enum migration_state_t { MIGRATION_STATE_NONE, MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED, MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE }
Migration detection and failover state. enum discovery_state_t { DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS, DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED }
Discovery session state.

Functions


discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const discovery_config_t *config)
Create a new discovery session. void discovery_session_destroy (discovery_session_t *session)
Destroy discovery session and free resources. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start (discovery_session_t *session)
Start the discovery session. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process (discovery_session_t *session, int64_t timeout_ns)
Process session events (call in main loop). void discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t *session)
Stop the discovery session. discovery_state_t discovery_session_get_state (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get current session state. bool discovery_session_is_active (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if session is active (call in progress). const char * discovery_session_get_string (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get session string. bool discovery_session_is_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the host. session_host_t * discovery_session_get_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Get host context (if we are host). session_participant_t * discovery_session_get_participant (discovery_session_t *session)
Get participant context (if we are participant). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_init_ring (discovery_session_t *session)
Initialize ring consensus state. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start_ring_round (discovery_session_t *session)
Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new joiner). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive (discovery_session_t *session)
Detect host disconnect (check connection status). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect (discovery_session_t *session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)
Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Become the host (called when elected as future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_connect_to_future_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Connect to pre-elected future host (called when NOT future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_get_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session, uint8_t out_id[16], char out_address[64], uint16_t *out_port, uint8_t *out_connection_type)
Get future host information. bool discovery_session_is_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the future host.

Definition at line 34 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)

Callback to check if session should exit. Called periodically during blocking operations (e.g., ACDS connect, join). Should return true to gracefully cancel the current operation.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

Returns

true to cancel operation, false to continue

Definition at line 112 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum discovery_state_t

Discovery session state.

Enumerator

DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT
Initial state.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS
Connecting to ACDS.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION
Creating new session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION
Joining existing session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER
Waiting for peer to join (initiator).
DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING
NAT negotiation in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST
Starting as host.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST
Connecting to host as participant.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE
Session active (call in progress).
DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING
Host migration in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED
Session failed.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED
Session ended.

enum migration_state_t


Migration detection and failover state. SIMPLIFIED DESIGN:
    No re-election during migration! Future host was pre-elected 5 minutes ago.
    When current host dies, we already know who takes over and where to find
    them.


Enumerator
  





  
MIGRATION_STATE_NONE
    

  
No migration in progress.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED
    

  
Host disconnect detected.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER
    

  
Failing over to pre-elected future host.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE
    

  
Failover complete, call resumed.

89   MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, 
90   MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE  
91 } migration_state_t;

Parameters

Returns

2199                                                                               {
2200   if (!session) {
2201     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2202     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2203   }
2204 
2205   log_info("Starting as new host after migration (participant ID: %02x%02x...)", session->participant_id[0],
2206            session->participant_id[1]);
2207 
2208   // Get configured port (or use default)
2209   int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
2210 
2211   // Mark ourselves as host
2212   session->is_host = true;
2213 
2214   // Create host context if needed with configured port
2215   if (!session->host_ctx) {
2216     session_host_config_t hconfig = {
2217         .port = host_port,
2218         .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
2219         .max_clients = 32,
2220         .encryption_enabled = true,
2221     };
2222 
2223     session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
2224     if (!session->host_ctx) {
2225       log_error("Failed to create host context for migration");
2226       return ERROR_MEMORY;
2227     }
2228 
2229     // Start listening for new participant connections
2230     asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
2231     if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2232       log_error("Failed to start host after migration: %d", hstart);
2233       return hstart;
2234     }
2235 
2236     log_info("Host restarted after migration, listening on port %d", host_port);
2237   }
2238 
2239   // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so other participants can reconnect
2240   acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
2241   memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2242   memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2243   SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(announcement.host_address));
2244   // TODO: Use actual determined host address instead of 127.0.0.1
2245   announcement.host_port = host_port;
2246   announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
2247   // TODO: Use actual connection type based on NAT quality
2248 
2249   if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2250     packet_send(session->acds_socket, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement, sizeof(announcement));
2251     log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS for new host %02x%02x... at %s:%u", announcement.host_id[0],
2252              announcement.host_id[1], announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
2253   }
2254 
2255   // Mark migration complete
2256   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2257 
2258   // Transition to ACTIVE state (host is ready)
2259   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2260 
2261   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2262 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Detect host disconnect (check connection status).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if host alive, error if disconnected


Definition at line 2076 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2076                                                                                    {
2077   if (!session || session->is_host) {
2078     // We are the host, so "host" is always alive
2079     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2080   }
2081 
2082   // For WebRTC sessions, check the WebRTC transport instead of participant_ctx
2083   if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2084     // If we have a WebRTC transport and it's marked as ready, host is alive
2085     if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
2086       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2087     }
2088     // WebRTC transport not ready yet - might still be connecting
2089     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2090   }
2091 
2092   // For TCP sessions, check participant context
2093   // If we don't have a participant context yet, we're not connected
2094   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2095     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2096   }
2097 
2098   // Check migration state - if we're already migrating, host is definitely not alive
2099   if (session->migration.state != MIGRATION_STATE_NONE) {
2100     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Already detected disconnect
2101   }
2102 
2103   uint64_t now_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2104 
2105   // Check if we have a ping in flight that timed out
2106   if (session->liveness.ping_in_flight) {
2107     uint64_t ping_age_ms = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2108     if (ping_age_ms > session->liveness.timeout_ms) {
2109       // Ping timed out
2110       session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2111       session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
2112       log_warn("Host ping timeout (attempt %u/%u, age=%llu ms)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures,
2113                session->liveness.max_failures, (unsigned long long)ping_age_ms);
2114 
2115       // Check if we've exceeded failure threshold
2116       if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2117         log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive ping failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2118         return ERROR_NETWORK;
2119       }
2120     }
2121   }
2122 
2123   // Check if it's time to send a new ping
2124   uint64_t time_since_last_ping = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2125   if (!session->liveness.ping_in_flight && time_since_last_ping >= session->liveness.ping_interval_ms) {
2126     // Send ping to host via participant connection
2127     socket_t host_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
2128     if (host_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2129       asciichat_error_t result = packet_send(host_socket, PACKET_TYPE_PING, NULL, 0);
2130       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2131         session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = now_ms;
2132         session->liveness.ping_in_flight = true;
2133         log_debug("Sent ping to host (attempt %u/%u)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures + 1,
2134                   session->liveness.max_failures);
2135       } else {
2136         log_warn("Failed to send ping to host: %d", result);
2137         session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2138         if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2139           log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive send failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2140           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2141         }
2142       }
2143     }
2144   }
2145 
2146   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2147 }

2275     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
2276     session->participant_ctx = NULL;

2278

2299   }
2300 
2301   log_info("Connected to future host after migration");
2302

2304   memcpy(session->host_id, session->ring.future_host_id, 16);

2306   session->host_port = session->ring.future_host_port;

  discovery_config_t * config)


Create a new discovery session.


Parameters


Config Module Session
  configuration


Returns


New session or NULL on error


Definition at line 50 of file

50                                                                                 {
51   if (!config) {
52     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
53     return NULL;
54   }
55 
56   discovery_session_t *session = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(discovery_session_t), discovery_session_t *);
57   if (!session) {
58     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate discovery session");
59     return NULL;
60   }
61 
62   session->state = DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT;
63   session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
64   session->peer_manager = NULL;
65   session->webrtc_transport_ready = false;
66   session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
67   session->webrtc_retry_attempt = 0;
68   session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = 0;
69   session->stun_servers = NULL;
70   session->stun_count = 0;
71   session->turn_servers = NULL;
72   session->turn_count = 0;
73 
74   // Initialize host liveness detection
75   session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = 0;
76   session->liveness.last_pong_received_ms = 0;
77   session->liveness.consecutive_failures = 0;
78   session->liveness.max_failures = 3;
79   session->liveness.ping_interval_ms = 3 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Ping every 3 seconds
80   session->liveness.timeout_ms = 10 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;      // 10 second timeout
81   session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
82 
83   log_info("discovery_session_create: After CALLOC - participant_ctx=%p, host_ctx=%p", session->participant_ctx,
84            session->host_ctx);
85 
86   // Load or generate identity key
87   char identity_path[256];
88   asciichat_error_t id_result = acds_identity_default_path(identity_path, sizeof(identity_path));
89   if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
90     id_result = acds_identity_load(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
91     if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
92       // File doesn't exist or is corrupted - generate new key
93       log_info("Identity key not found, generating new key");
94       id_result = acds_identity_generate(session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
95       if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
96         // Save for future use
97         acds_identity_save(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
98       }
99     }
100   }
101 
102   if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
103     log_warn("Failed to load/generate identity key, using zero key");
104     memset(session->identity_pubkey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_pubkey));
105     memset(session->identity_seckey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_seckey));
106   } else {
107     char fingerprint[65];
108     acds_identity_fingerprint(session->identity_pubkey, fingerprint);
109     log_info("Using identity key with fingerprint: %.16s...", fingerprint);
110   }
111 
112   // Load multiple identity keys from options (multi-key protocol support)
113   const options_t *opts = options_get();
114   if (opts && opts->num_identity_keys > 0) {
115     // Load all identity keys from options
116     for (size_t i = 0; i < opts->num_identity_keys && i < MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS; i++) {
117       uint8_t pubkey[32] = {0};
118       uint8_t seckey[64] = {0};
119       asciichat_error_t key_result = acds_identity_load(opts->identity_keys[i], pubkey, seckey);
120       if (key_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
121         memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[i], pubkey, 32);
122         char fingerprint[65];
123         acds_identity_fingerprint(pubkey, fingerprint);
124         log_info("Loaded identity key #%zu with fingerprint: %.16s...", i + 1, fingerprint);
125       } else {
126         log_warn("Failed to load identity key #%zu from %s", i + 1, opts->identity_keys[i]);
127       }
128     }
129     session->num_identity_keys = opts->num_identity_keys;
130     log_info("Loaded %zu identity key(s) for multi-key protocol", session->num_identity_keys);
131   } else {
132     // Single-key mode: use only the primary identity key
133     memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[0], session->identity_pubkey, 32);
134     session->num_identity_keys = 1;
135   }
136 
137   // ACDS connection info
138   if (config->acds_address && config->acds_address[0]) {
139     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, config->acds_address, sizeof(session->acds_address));
140   } else {
141     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->acds_address));
142   }
143   session->acds_port = config->acds_port > 0 ? config->acds_port : ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT;
144 
145   // Session string (if joining)
146   if (config->session_string && config->session_string[0]) {
147     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->session_string, config->session_string, sizeof(session->session_string));
148     session->is_initiator = false;
149   } else {
150     session->is_initiator = true;
151   }
152 
153   // Callbacks
154   session->on_state_change = config->on_state_change;
155   session->on_session_ready = config->on_session_ready;
156   session->on_error = config->on_error;
157   session->callback_user_data = config->callback_user_data;
158   session->should_exit_callback = config->should_exit_callback;
159   session->exit_callback_data = config->exit_callback_data;
160 
161   log_debug("Discovery session created (initiator=%d, acds=%s:%u)", session->is_initiator, session->acds_address,
162             session->acds_port);
163 
164   return session;
165 }

175     socket_close(session->acds_socket);
176     session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
177   }
178 
179   // Destroy host context if active
180   if (session->host_ctx) {
181     session_host_destroy(session->host_ctx);
182     session->host_ctx = NULL;

184 
185   // Destroy participant context if active
186   if (session->participant_ctx) {
187     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
188     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
189   }
190 
191   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (NEW)
192   if (session->peer_manager) {
193     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
194     session->peer_manager = NULL;
195   }
196 
197   // Clean up STUN/TURN server arrays (NEW)
198   if (session->stun_servers) {
199     SAFE_FREE(session->stun_servers);
200     session->stun_servers = NULL;
201     session->stun_count = 0;
202   }
203 
204   if (session->turn_servers) {
205     SAFE_FREE(session->turn_servers);
206     session->turn_servers = NULL;
207     session->turn_count = 0;
208   }
209 
210   SAFE_FREE(session);
211 }

  (discovery_session_t * session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)


Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host.


Parameters


Session Session context


disconnect_reason Reason code for disconnect


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on successful failover


Definition at line 2149 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2149                                                                                                                      {
2150   if (!session) {
2151     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2152     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2153   }
2154 
2155   log_warn("Host disconnect detected: reason=%u", disconnect_reason);
2156 
2157   // Initialize migration context
2158   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED;
2159   session->migration.detection_time_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2160   memcpy(session->migration.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2161   session->migration.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2162 
2163   // Notify ACDS of host loss (lightweight notification, no NAT data exchanged!)
2164   acip_host_lost_t host_lost = {0};
2165   memcpy(host_lost.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2166   memcpy(host_lost.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2167   memcpy(host_lost.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2168   host_lost.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2169   host_lost.disconnect_time_ms = session->migration.detection_time_ms;
2170 
2171   packet_send(session->acds_socket, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_LOST, &host_lost, sizeof(host_lost));
2172 
2173   // Check if we have a pre-elected future host (from last 5-minute ring round)
2174   if (session->ring.future_host_id[0] == 0) {
2175     // No future host known! This shouldn't happen if we just received FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED
2176     // within the last 5 minutes. Fall back to treating this as fatal.
2177     log_error("No future host pre-elected! Session cannot recover from host disconnect.");
2178     session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2179     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Session should end
2180   }
2181 
2182   // **SIMPLIFIED**: No metrics exchange needed! Future host was already elected 5 minutes ago.
2183   // Just failover to the pre-elected host.
2184   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER;
2185 
2186   // Check if I am the future host
2187   if (session->ring.am_future_host) {
2188     // I become the new host immediately! (no election delay!)
2189     log_info("Becoming host (I'm the pre-elected future host)");
2190     return discovery_session_become_host(session);
2191   } else {
2192     // Connect to pre-elected future host (address already stored!)
2193     log_info("Connecting to pre-elected future host: %s:%u", session->ring.future_host_address,
2194              session->ring.future_host_port);
2195     return discovery_session_connect_to_future_host(session);
2196   }
2197 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process
  (discovery_session_t * session, int64_t timeout_ns)


Process session events (call in main loop). Handles incoming ACDS
    messages, negotiation, and state transitions.




Parameters


Session Session context


timeout_ns Max time to wait for events in nanoseconds (0 =
  non-blocking)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error on failure


Definition at line 1375 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1375                                                                                               {
1376   if (!session) {
1377     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1378   }
1379 
1380   // Handle state-specific processing
1381   switch (session->state) {
1382   case DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER: {
1383     // Wait for PARTICIPANT_JOINED notification from ACDS
1384     if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE && timeout_ns > 0) {
1385       // Set socket timeout
1386       if (socket_set_timeout(session->acds_socket, timeout_ns) == 0) {
1387         packet_type_t type;
1388         void *data = NULL;
1389         size_t len = 0;
1390 
1391         // Try to receive a packet (non-blocking with timeout)
1392         asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive(session->acds_socket, &type, &data, &len);
1393         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1394           if (type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED) {
1395             log_info("Peer joined session, transitioning to negotiation");
1396             set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING);
1397             POOL_FREE(data, len);
1398           } else {
1399             // Got some other packet, handle it or ignore
1400             log_debug("Received packet type %d while waiting for peer", type);
1401             POOL_FREE(data, len);
1402           }
1403         } else if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1404           // Timeout is normal, just return
1405         } else {
1406           log_debug("Packet receive error while waiting for peer: %d", recv_result);
1407         }
1408       }
1409     } else {
1410       // Fallback to sleep if no timeout or invalid socket
1411       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1412     }
1413     break;
1414   }
1415 
1416   case DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING: {
1417     // Handle NAT negotiation
1418     if (session->negotiate.state == NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT) {
1419       // Initialize negotiation
1420       negotiate_init(&session->negotiate, session->session_id, session->participant_id, session->is_initiator);
1421       log_info("Starting NAT negotiation (initiator=%d)", session->is_initiator);
1422 
1423       // Send our NETWORK_QUALITY to ACDS immediately (let peer receive it)
1424       asciichat_error_t send_result = send_network_quality_to_acds(session);
1425       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1426         log_warn("Failed to send initial NETWORK_QUALITY: %d", send_result);
1427         // Don't fail - we'll try again next iteration
1428       }
1429     }
1430 
1431     // Try to receive peer's NETWORK_QUALITY from ACDS (non-blocking with short timeout)
1432     if (!session->negotiate.peer_quality_received) {
1433       asciichat_error_t recv_result = receive_network_quality_from_acds(session);
1434       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1435         // Got peer quality - proceed to election
1436         log_info("Received peer NETWORK_QUALITY, proceeding to host election");
1437       } else if (recv_result != ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1438         // Real error (not just timeout)
1439         log_debug("Error receiving NETWORK_QUALITY: %d (this might be normal if peer not ready)", recv_result);
1440       }
1441     }
1442 
1443     // Check if we have both qualities and can determine host
1444     if (session->negotiate.peer_quality_received && session->negotiate.state != NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING) {
1445       // Both sides have exchanged NAT quality - determine the winner
1446       log_info("Both NAT qualities received, determining host...");
1447       asciichat_error_t result = negotiate_determine_result(&session->negotiate);
1448       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1449         // Store result
1450         session->is_host = session->negotiate.we_are_host;
1451 
1452         if (session->is_host) {
1453           // We won the negotiation - become the host
1454           log_info("🏠 NAT negotiation complete: WE ARE HOST (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1455                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1456                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1457           memcpy(session->host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1458           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1459           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1460           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST);
1461         } else {
1462           // They won - we'll be a participant
1463           log_info("👤 NAT negotiation complete: THEY ARE HOST at %s:%u (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1464                    session->negotiate.host_address, session->negotiate.host_port,
1465                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1466                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1467           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1468           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1469           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST);
1470         }
1471       } else {
1472         log_error("Failed to determine negotiation result: %d", result);
1473         set_error(session, result, "Host election failed");
1474       }
1475     } else {
1476       // Still waiting for peer quality - sleep briefly to avoid busy loop
1477       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1478     }
1479     break;
1480   }
1481 
1482   case DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST: {
1483     // Start hosting
1484     if (!session->host_ctx) {
1485       // Create host context with configured port
1486       int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1487 
1488       session_host_config_t hconfig = {
1489           .port = host_port,
1490           .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
1491           .max_clients = 32,
1492           .encryption_enabled = true,
1493       };
1494 
1495       session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
1496       if (!session->host_ctx) {
1497         log_error("Failed to create host context");
1498         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create host context");
1499         break;
1500       }
1501 
1502       // Start listening
1503       asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
1504       if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1505         log_error("Failed to start host: %d", hstart);
1506         set_error(session, hstart, "Failed to start host");
1507         break;
1508       }
1509 
1510       log_info("Host started, listening for connections");
1511     }
1512 
1513     // Notify listeners that we're ready
1514     if (session->on_session_ready) {
1515       session->on_session_ready(session->session_string, session->callback_user_data);
1516     }
1517 
1518     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1519     break;
1520   }
1521 
1522   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST: {
1523     // Connect to host as participant
1524 
1525     // For WebRTC sessions, wait for DataChannel to open
1526     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
1527       // Check if transport is ready (DataChannel opened)
1528       if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
1529         log_info("WebRTC DataChannel established, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1530         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1531         break;
1532       }
1533 
1534       // If not ready and we haven't initiated connection yet, initiate it
1535       if (session->peer_manager && !session->webrtc_connection_initiated) {
1536         log_info("Initiating WebRTC connection to host (attempt %d/%d)...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1537                  GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts) + 1);
1538 
1539         // Initiate connection (generates offer, triggers SDP exchange)
1540         asciichat_error_t conn_result =
1541             webrtc_peer_manager_connect(session->peer_manager, session->session_id, session->host_id);
1542 
1543         if (conn_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1544           log_error("Failed to initiate WebRTC connection: %d", conn_result);
1545           set_error(session, conn_result, "Failed to initiate WebRTC connection");
1546           break;
1547         }
1548 
1549         log_info("WebRTC connection initiated, waiting for DataChannel...");
1550         session->webrtc_connection_initiated = true;
1551         session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() / 1000;
1552       }
1553 
1554       // Check for ICE gathering timeout on all peers
1555       if (session->webrtc_connection_initiated && session->peer_manager) {
1556         int timeout_ms = GET_OPTION(webrtc_ice_timeout_ms);
1557         int timed_out_count = webrtc_peer_manager_check_gathering_timeouts(session->peer_manager, timeout_ms);
1558 
1559         if (timed_out_count > 0) {
1560           log_error("ICE gathering timeout: %d peer(s) failed to gather candidates within %dms", timed_out_count,
1561                     timeout_ms);
1562 
1563           // Check if we have retries remaining
1564           int max_attempts = GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts);
1565           if (session->webrtc_retry_attempt < max_attempts) {
1566             // Calculate exponential backoff delay
1567             uint32_t backoff_ms = calculate_backoff_delay_ms(session->webrtc_retry_attempt);
1568 
1569             log_warn("WebRTC connection attempt %d/%d failed, retrying in %ums...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1570                      max_attempts + 1, backoff_ms);
1571 
1572             // Wait for backoff period
1573             platform_sleep_ms(backoff_ms);
1574 
1575             // Destroy old peer manager
1576             webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
1577             session->peer_manager = NULL;
1578 
1579             // Re-initialize peer manager for retry
1580             asciichat_error_t reinit_result = initialize_webrtc_peer_manager(session);
1581             if (reinit_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1582               log_error("Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry: %d", reinit_result);
1583               set_error(session, reinit_result, "Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry");
1584 
1585               // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error
1586               if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1587                 log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1588                 set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1589                 return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1590               }
1591 
1592               break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1593             }
1594 
1595             // Reset connection state for retry
1596             session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
1597             session->webrtc_retry_attempt++;
1598 
1599             log_info("WebRTC peer manager re-initialized for attempt %d/%d", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1600                      max_attempts + 1);
1601           } else {
1602             // No more retries - connection failed
1603             log_error("WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (timeout: %dms)", max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1604 
1605             // Set error state - WebRTC connection failed
1606             char error_msg[256];
1607             snprintf(error_msg, sizeof(error_msg), "WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (%dms timeout)",
1608                      max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1609             set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, error_msg);
1610 
1611             // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error (no TCP fallback)
1612             if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1613               log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1614               set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1615               return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1616             }
1617 
1618             break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1619           }
1620         }
1621       }
1622 
1623       // Poll ACDS for WebRTC signaling messages (SDP answer, ICE candidates)
1624       // while waiting for DataChannel to open
1625       if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1626         // Check if data available (non-blocking with timeout_ns)
1627         struct timeval tv_timeout;
1628         tv_timeout.tv_sec = timeout_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
1629         tv_timeout.tv_usec = (timeout_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT) / 1000;
1630 
1631         fd_set readfds;
1632         FD_ZERO(&readfds);
1633         FD_SET(session->acds_socket, &readfds);
1634 
1635         int select_result = select(session->acds_socket + 1, &readfds, NULL, NULL, &tv_timeout);
1636         if (select_result > 0 && FD_ISSET(session->acds_socket, &readfds)) {
1637           // Data available to read
1638           packet_type_t type;
1639           void *data = NULL;
1640           size_t len = 0;
1641 
1642           asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive(session->acds_socket, &type, &data, &len);
1643 
1644           if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1645             // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1646             handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1647             POOL_FREE(data, len);
1648           } else {
1649             log_debug("Failed to receive ACDS packet: %d", recv_result);
1650           }
1651         }
1652       }
1653 
1654       // Stay in CONNECTING_HOST until DataChannel opens (webrtc_transport_ready becomes true)
1655       // The discovery_on_transport_ready callback will set webrtc_transport_ready=true
1656       break;
1657     }
1658 
1659     if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1660       // Create participant context for this session
1661       session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
1662           .address = session->host_address,
1663           .port = session->host_port,
1664           .enable_audio = true,
1665           .enable_video = true,
1666           .encryption_enabled = true,
1667       };
1668 
1669       session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
1670       if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1671         log_error("Failed to create participant context");
1672         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create participant context");
1673         break;
1674       }
1675 
1676       // Attempt connection
1677       asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
1678       if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1679         log_error("Failed to connect as participant: %d", pconn);
1680         // Stay in this state, will retry on next process() call
1681         break;
1682       }
1683 
1684       log_info("Connected to host, performing crypto handshake...");
1685 
1686       // Perform crypto handshake with server
1687       // Get the socket from participant context
1688       socket_t participant_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
1689       if (participant_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1690         log_error("Failed to get socket from participant context");
1691         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to get participant socket");
1692         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1693         break;
1694       }
1695 
1696       // Step 1: Send client protocol version
1697       log_debug("Sending protocol version to server...");
1698       protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
1699       client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
1700       client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
1701       client_version.supports_encryption = 1;                // We support encryption
1702       client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
1703       client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
1704       client_version.feature_flags = 0;
1705 
1706       int result = send_protocol_version_packet(participant_socket, &client_version);
1707       if (result != 0) {
1708         log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
1709         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send protocol version");
1710         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1711         break;
1712       }
1713 
1714       // Step 2: Receive server protocol version
1715       log_debug("Receiving server protocol version...");
1716       packet_type_t packet_type;
1717       void *payload = NULL;
1718       size_t payload_len = 0;
1719 
1720       int recv_result = receive_packet(participant_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
1721       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1722         log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type 0x%x)", packet_type);
1723         if (payload) {
1724           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1725         }
1726         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive protocol version from server");
1727         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1728         break;
1729       }
1730 
1731       if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
1732         log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu", payload_len);
1733         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1734         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid protocol version packet");
1735         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1736         break;
1737       }
1738 
1739       protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
1740       memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
1741       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1742 
1743       if (!server_version.supports_encryption) {
1744         log_error("Server does not support encryption");
1745         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Server does not support encryption");
1746         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1747         break;
1748       }
1749 
1750       log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (encryption: yes)", NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version),
1751                NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision));
1752 
1753       // Step 3: Send crypto capabilities
1754       log_debug("Sending crypto capabilities...");
1755       crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
1756       client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001);    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1757       client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0003);   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE
1758       client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001); // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1759       client_caps.requires_verification = 0;
1760       client_caps.preferred_kex = 0x0001;    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1761       client_caps.preferred_auth = 0x0001;   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519
1762       client_caps.preferred_cipher = 0x0001; // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1763 
1764       result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(participant_socket, &client_caps);
1765       if (result != 0) {
1766         log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1767         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1768         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1769         break;
1770       }
1771 
1772       log_info("Crypto handshake initiated successfully");
1773       log_warn("*** Connected to host as participant - transitioning to ACTIVE ***");
1774     }
1775 
1776     // Check if connection is established
1777     bool ctx_valid = session->participant_ctx != NULL;
1778     bool is_connected = ctx_valid ? session_participant_is_connected(session->participant_ctx) : false;
1779     log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - participant_ctx=%p, ctx_valid=%d, is_connected=%d",
1780               session->participant_ctx, ctx_valid, is_connected);
1781 
1782     if (ctx_valid && !is_connected) {
1783       // Log more details about why it's not connected
1784       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - context exists but not connected yet");
1785     }
1786 
1787     if (is_connected) {
1788       log_info("Participant connection confirmed, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1789       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1790     } else {
1791       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - awaiting connection establishment (ctx_valid=%d, "
1792                 "is_connected=%d)",
1793                 ctx_valid, is_connected);
1794     }
1795     break;
1796   }
1797 
1798   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE:
1799     // Receive and handle ACDS packets for WebRTC signaling (NEW)
1800     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC && session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1801       // Use non-blocking polling to check for incoming ACDS packets
1802       struct pollfd pfd = {.fd = session->acds_socket, .events = POLLIN, .revents = 0};
1803 
1804       int poll_result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, 0); // Non-blocking poll (timeout=0)
1805 
1806       if (poll_result > 0 && (pfd.revents & POLLIN)) {
1807         // Data available to read
1808         packet_type_t type;
1809         void *data = NULL;
1810         size_t len = 0;
1811 
1812         asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive(session->acds_socket, &type, &data, &len);
1813 
1814         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1815           // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1816           handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1817           POOL_FREE(data, len);
1818         }
1819       }
1820     }
1821 
1822     // Session is active - check for host disconnect
1823     if (!session->is_host) {
1824       // We are a participant - check if host is still alive
1825       asciichat_error_t host_check = discovery_session_check_host_alive(session);
1826       if (host_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1827         // Host is down! Trigger automatic failover to pre-elected future host
1828         log_warn("Host disconnect detected during ACTIVE state");
1829 
1830         // If --prefer-webrtc is set, WebRTC failure is fatal (no TCP fallback)
1831         const options_t *opts = options_get();
1832         if (opts && opts->prefer_webrtc) {
1833           log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1834           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1835           session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
1836           return ERROR_NETWORK;
1837         }
1838 
1839         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING);
1840         // Reason code: 1 = timeout/disconnect detected during ACTIVE session
1841         discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect(session, 1);
1842       }
1843     }
1844     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1845     break;
1846 
1847   case DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING:
1848     // Host migration in progress
1849     // Check if migration has timed out (if it takes >30 seconds, give up)
1850     if (session->migration.state == MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE) {
1851       // Migration completed successfully
1852       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1853       log_info("Migration complete, session resumed");
1854     } else if (session_get_current_time_ms() - session->migration.detection_time_ms > 30 * MS_PER_SEC_INT) {
1855       // Migration timed out after 30 seconds
1856       log_error("Host migration timeout - session cannot recover");
1857       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1858       session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
1859     }
1860     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1861     break;
1862 
1863   default:
1864     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid session state");
1865     break;
1866   }
1867 
1868   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1869 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Start the discovery session. Connects to ACDS and either creates
    or joins a session.




Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success

1271                                                                         {
1272   log_info("discovery_session_start: ENTRY - session=%p", session);
1273 
1274   if (!session) {
1275     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1276   }
1277 
1278   log_info("discovery_session_start: is_initiator=%d, session_string='%s'", session->is_initiator,
1279            session->session_string);
1280 
1281   // Check if we should exit before starting blocking operations
1282   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1283     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (before ACDS connect)");
1284     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1285   }
1286 
1287   log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling connect_to_acds...");
1288   // Connect to ACDS
1289   asciichat_error_t result = connect_to_acds(session);
1290   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1291     log_error("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds FAILED - result=%d", result);
1292     return result;
1293   }
1294   log_info("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds succeeded");
1295 
1296   // Check again after connection
1297   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1298     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after ACDS connect)");
1299     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1300   }
1301 
1302   // Perform crypto handshake with ACDS (same as client-server handshake)
1303   log_info("discovery_session_start: Performing crypto handshake with ACDS...");
1304   crypto_handshake_context_t handshake_ctx;
1305   memset(&handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(handshake_ctx));
1306 
1307   result = crypto_handshake_init("discovery_client", &handshake_ctx, false);
1308   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1309     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake init failed - %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1310     set_error(session, result, "Crypto handshake init failed");
1311     return result;
1312   }
1313 
1314   // Store identity key in handshake context
1315   handshake_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1316   handshake_ctx.client_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1317   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_public_key.key, session->identity_pubkey, 32);
1318   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_private_key.key.ed25519, session->identity_seckey, 64);
1319 
1320   // Step 1: Key exchange
1321   result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange_socket(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_socket);
1322   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1323     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake key exchange failed");
1324     set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange failed");
1325     return result;
1326   }
1327 
1328   // Step 2: Auth response
1329   // Note: For ACDS servers without authentication, auth_response completes the handshake
1330   result = crypto_handshake_client_auth_response_socket(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_socket);
1331   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1332     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake auth response failed");
1333     set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response failed");
1334     return result;
1335   }
1336 
1337   // Step 3: Complete handshake
1338   // Note: Some servers (like ACDS without auth) complete the handshake in step 2,
1339   // so check if we're already done before trying to receive another packet
1340   if (handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1341     result = crypto_handshake_client_complete_socket(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_socket);
1342     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1343       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake complete failed");
1344       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete failed");
1345       return result;
1346     }
1347   } else {
1348     log_info("discovery_session_start: Handshake already complete from auth_response step");
1349   }
1350 
1351   log_info("discovery_session_start: Crypto handshake with ACDS completed");
1352 
1353   // Check again after handshake
1354   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1355     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after handshake)");
1356     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1357   }
1358 
1359   // Create or join session
1360   if (session->is_initiator) {
1361     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling create_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1362     result = create_session(session);
1363     log_info("discovery_session_start: create_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, result=%d",
1364              session->participant_ctx, result);
1365     return result;
1366   } else {
1367     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling join_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1368     asciichat_error_t result = join_session(session);
1369     log_info("discovery_session_start: join_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, state=%d, result=%d",
1370              session->participant_ctx, session->state, result);
1371     return result;
1372   }
1373 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start_ring_round
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new
    joiner).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 2047 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2047                                                                                    {
2048   if (!session) {
2049     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2050     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2051   }
2052 
2053   session->ring.last_ring_round_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2054 
2055   if (session->is_host) {
2056     log_info("Starting 5-minute proactive election round (collecting NETWORK_QUALITY from participants)");
2057 
2058     // HOST: Run proactive host election
2059     // 1. Collect NETWORK_QUALITY from all connected participants (TODO: request fresh data)
2060     // 2. Measure the host's own NAT quality
2061     // 3. Run deterministic election to determine future host
2062     // 4. Broadcast FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED to all participants via ACDS

2069     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2070   } else {
2071     log_debug("Ring round timer triggered (waiting for host to broadcast FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED)");
2072     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2073   }
2074 }

void
  discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t * session)


Stop the discovery session.


Parameters


Session Session context

1872   if (!session) {
1873     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1874     return;
1875   }
1876 
1877   log_info("Stopping discovery session...");
1878 
1879   // Send SESSION_LEAVE if connected
1880   if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE && session->session_id[0] != 0) {
1881     acip_session_leave_t leave_msg;
1882     memset(&leave_msg, 0, sizeof(leave_msg));
1883     memcpy(leave_msg.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
1884     memcpy(leave_msg.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1885 
1886     packet_send(session->acds_socket, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_LEAVE, &leave_msg, sizeof(leave_msg));
1887   }
1888
